I treated the prior turns as a research log, not as proof. The only still-unrefuted candidate remains the dynamic tame-inertia word; the earlier scalar, plain-œÄ2, and fixed-œÑ‚àí2 variants have recorded finite-quotient failures in the prior log. The prompt‚Äôs required format is the special tame presentation with distinguished œÉ,œÑ, implicit œÑœÉ=œÑ2, later pro-2-core generators, and exponents modulo M=85667662080.

The live candidate is still:variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently,r=xœÉ‚àíœÄxœÉœÄx‚àí1(xœÑ‚àíŒ∑)œÄ ((yœÑ)œÄ)œÉy‚àí1,œÄ=œÄ2,Œ∑=2œÄ,with œÄ‚â°40491355905,‚àíœÄ‚â°45176306175,‚àíŒ∑‚â°50932630783(modM).I did not find a new counterexample. My new contribution is a sharper reduction of the remaining first-IA arithmetic comparison: the primitive anti-cyclotomic tests can be ordered and minimized, and the next tests should be smaller than some prior turns suggested.The stable conventions from the later turns should be kept in equation form, because several prior turns alternated between reciprocal notations. For a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character œÑ‚Ü¶Œ±ÓÄÝ=1 in the sign double cover with deck involution t, the norm coefficientDa=j=0‚àëœÄ‚àí1(tŒ±a)j satisfies (1‚àítŒ±a)Da=1‚àí(tŒ±a)œÄ=1‚àít,henceDa=(1‚àít)(1‚àítŒ±a)‚àí1.

This is the direction that makes Da radical and ‚àí1+D‚àíŒ∑ a unit.

In the trivial tame-inertia block, the safe convention is:(1+T)Ps (T)=1+T‚àís,s‚â°œÄ(modf),where T is the œÉ-cycle operator. This is the polynomial elimination Y=Ps (T)X+YÀâ; using the reciprocal in F2 [T]/(Tf‚àí1) is not legitimate. With these conventions, the prior conclusionsUe,fab‚âÉZ2ef+1‚äïC2,(kerŒ∫)ab‚âÉZ22ef+1‚äïC4 remain internally consistent.Here is the new reduction. For a finite tame quotientŒîe,f=‚ü®œÉÀâ,œÑÀâ‚à£œÑÀâe=1,œÉÀâf=1,œÉÀâœÑÀâœÉÀâ‚àí1=œÑÀâ2‚ü©,write f=2n for the even-f case. The first mixed IA directions are indexed by involutionsœÑÀâjœÉÀân,and(œÑÀâjœÉÀân)2=œÑÀâ j(1+2n).

Thus the number of mixed directions is d=gcd(e,2n+1).Equivalently, the first-IA mixed residual depends only on the anti-cyclotomic divisord=e‚àí:=gcd(e,2f/2+1),not on the complementary divisor e+=gcd(e,2f/2‚àí1). The complementary tame-inertia factor is first-IA-free at this layer.This means that a primitive exact-order c residual can first appear only when2n ‚â°‚àí1(modc).Let n(c) be the least positive n with this property, if it exists. Then the smallest finite tame quotient carrying a primitive c-obstruction is (e,f)=(c,2n(c)).If no such n(c) exists, then c never appears as a primitive anti-cyclotomic first-IA obstruction.This gives a concrete priority list for arithmetic residual tests, ordered by tame degree ef:primitive¬Ýexact¬Ýorder¬Ýc35911171333192725 minimal¬Ýf2461081210181820ef62054110136156330342486500dimF2EcAc=œÜ(c)24610161220181820 So after the current proposed (9,6) computation, the next primitive first-IA test by degree is (11,10), not (17,8) or (91,12). Also, (91,12) is not primitive-91 at this layer: since 26+1=65, its anti-cyclotomic divisor is gcd(91,65)=13.Thus its first-IA mixed part is pulled from the d=13 test; the extra 7-part is invisible at this first IA layer.

For the immediate (9,6) test, letA9=F2 [T]/(T9‚àí1),R(T)=i=0‚àë8riTi be the arithmetic-minus-candidate mixed residual after all gauges are fixed. The exact-order projectors give a triangular coefficient test:

The primitive 9-part vanishes iffr0=r3=r6,r1=r4=r7,r2=r5=r8.If this fails, it is a genuinely primitive 9-obstruction.

If it passes, write the descended C3 -residual asR3=a+bU+cU2 with a=r0=r3=r6, b=r1=r4=r7, c=r2=r5=r8. The exact-order 3-part vanishes iff a=b=c.The exact-order 1-part then vanishes iff the common value is 0.

So the full (9,6) first-IA target is:œµb=œµS=0,plus the three coefficient conditions above. This is a more explicit arithmetic checklist than simply saying ‚Äúapply E1,E3,E9.‚Äù This does not prove the presentation. It sharpens the remaining obstruction: the candidate is still alive, but the missing work is an arithmetic comparison showing that the actual local Galois action has zero arithmetic-minus-candidate residual in these normalized anti-cyclotomic components.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. I refined the first-IA comparison: for even f=2n, the mixed residual depends only on d=gcd(e,2n+1). Primitive exact-order c tests first occur at the minimal quotient (e,f)=(c,2n(c)) with 2n(c)‚â°‚àí1(modc). This gives a corrected priority list: after (9,6), the next primitive test by degree is (11,10), while (91,12) reduces at first IA to d=13. I also made the (9,6) coefficient test explicit.

GAP: The arithmetic-minus-candidate residual has still not been computed, even for (9,6). A dyadic formation theorem or explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic comparison is still needed to prove that the candidate‚Äôs full filtered tame action agrees with arithmetic over every finite tame subextension.

NEXT: Compute the (9,6) arithmetic residual for L=Q2(Œ∂9,œñ), œñ9=2, after fixing œÑ, a Frobenius lift, and a normal-basis gauge. Test œµb=œµS=0, then the primitive 9, descended 3, and order 1 coefficient conditions above. If all vanish, proceed by the priority list to (11,10), then (17,8), then (13,12).[BREAKTHROUGH]
